title: membership — field classification
description: Membership tables carry mostly internal-tier structural + financial data. Confidential fields include cancellation_reason (business-sensitive), failure_reason (payment gateway hints). Financial signals (price_snapshot_cents, lifetime_value_cents) are internal + audit-critical.

confidential: Membership.state_reason (admin-visible; can hint at legal/medical contexts)
Membership.cancellation_reason (customer wrote — business-sensitive; churn analytics + retention)
MembershipRenewal.failure_reason (payment gateway error text — may hint at card state; PCI-adjacent even without PAN)
regulated_minor: For memberships where linked Athlete is a minor: the membership surface inherits regulated_minor tier for cross-tenant reads.
audit_log: Membership.status (every transition — SOX 404 critical, 7 or 10 years retention)

notes: Membership tables carry NO PII directly. Athlete + Guardian PII is in the linked User + Athlete + AthleteGuardian modules. Cross-tenant regulated_minor stripping applies transitively.
Payment tokens (PAN + CVV) NEVER appear in these tables. Payment module owns PCI-DSS scope.
coupon_snapshot + tax_calculation_snapshot are FROZEN copies — even after the source records are hard-deleted, the snapshot preserves the audit trail.
MembershipRenewal.failure_reason is bounded to 256 chars + sanitized — never store gateway response bodies verbatim (may leak partial PAN or auth codes).
Cancellation reasons + business-sensitive analytics are hashed/bucketed before flowing to third-party analytics (Segment / posthog / etc.).
